summary: Backend-only regression for prod-outage fix (server.py restore, .gitignore .env unblock, CORS wildcard). All smoke checks pass: server imports cleanly, /api/company-info=200, supervisor RUNNING, no fresh tracebacks. CORS wildcard verified for both karyaai.app and random origin (returns access-control-allow-origin: * with allow-credentials ABSENT). All 6 security headers present. iter30 regression: 19/20 pass — the single failure (TestCompanyInfo.test_company_info_public) is a stale test expectation, NOT a regression: CONTACT_EMAIL env var is set to sixn8.technologies@gmail.com in backend/.env, so endpoint correctly returns that value; test hardcodes old default admin@dukaaon.in. Additional smoke: /api/cost-trends, /api/telegram/notifications, /api/expenses with q+limit all return 200. .gitignore no longer contains .env entries; git status shows backend/.env and frontend/.env as untracked (ready for deploy commit).

minor.issue: Stale test assertion in test_iteration30.py::TestCompanyInfo::test_company_info_public — expects support_email=='admin@dukaaon.in' but env CONTACT_EMAIL=sixn8.technologies@gmail.com. Endpoint behavior is correct; update the test expectation or align env to intended default.

action_items: Optional: update test_iteration30.py::test_company_info_public to read expected support_email from env, or set CONTACT_EMAIL back to admin@dukaaon.in if that was the intended default. Not blocking prod deploy.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses deprecated @app.on_event('startup') (2 occurrences at L2108, L4039) — migrate to lifespan handler in a future iteration.
server.py is very large (>4000 lines) — should be split into feature modules; user has already begun this with routes/reports.py, continue the pattern.
context_for_next_testing_agent: server.py restored and healthy. CORS=* with allow_credentials auto-disabled when wildcard detected. .gitignore no longer excludes .env files. Only outstanding issue is the stale support_email assertion in test_iteration30.py — cosmetic.
rca of the issue: Prod outage RCA: (1) backend/server.py was deleted from working tree causing uvicorn 'Could not import module server' on deploy; (2) .gitignore excluded .env so deploy image had no env vars; (3) CORS pinned to single domain. All three resolved: server.py restored via git checkout, .gitignore cleaned, CORS_ORIGINS=* with dynamic allow_credentials toggle in server.py. Verified all fixes in preview environment.
